similar_questions.title: Maximum Elegance of a K-Length Subsequence

discussion_posts.name: Finesse
discussion_posts.content: Will my LeetCoins be converted to stocks?

discussion_posts.name: Lyle_C
discussion_posts.content: The question should specify that you don\'t lose the capital when you use it for a project. The examples also don\'t do a good job illustrating that. But overall, this is one of the easier "Hard" problems

discussion_posts.name: deleted_user
discussion_posts.content: Shouldn\'t the capital be spent when you start the project? For example, if you have 1 capital and the project costs 1 capital with a profit of 3. Shouldn\'t the outcome be 3? Since you spend 1 and gain 3 (1 - 1 + 3)? Not sure why the question is phrased where you get to keep invested capital... Makes it more confusing tbh.

discussion_posts.name: rohan5
discussion_posts.content:
Most of these types of problems where we need to find min or max seem to require DP.

Eg:- Kanpsack Problem

I was wondering why this one can be solved by Greedy. 
I just wanted help to figure out DP vs. Greedy problems and this seems to be a good example.

Sorry I don't have code to show yet, maybe someone could help me out with that.
